results.description: DESCRIPTION:THIS PROJECT AIMS TO DEVELOP A COST-EFFECTIVE METHOD TO DEGRADE HARMFUL ALGAL BLOOMS (HABS) SUCH AS MICROCYSTIN LEUCINE ARGININE (MC-LR) IN RECREATIONAL AND DRINKING WATER SOURCES.ACTIVITIES:RESEARCHERS WILL USE AN INNOVATIVE GOLD (AU)-DECORATED METAL-ORGANIC FRAMEWORK (MOF) WHERE THE GOLD NANOPARTICLES PROVIDE THE SOLAR FUNCTION FOR THE PHOTOCATALYST. THE PROJECT'S THREE RESEARCH OBJECTIVES ARE: 1) DESIGN AND FABRICATE AN AU-DECORATED NI-MOF; 2) CHARACTERIZE AND EVALUATE OPTIMAL PARAMETER CONDITIONS; AND 3) CONDUCT A LIFE CYCLE ANALYSIS FOR MC-LR REMOVAL.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES WILL INCLUDE ANNUAL AND FINAL REPORTS ON THEIR EFFORT TO DESIGN AND FABRICATE A GOLD DECORATED PHOTOCATALYST (AU/NI MOF) FOR ITS ABILITY TO ADVANCE SUSTAINABLE WATER TREATMENT METHODS FOR THE REMOVAL OF MC-LR, WHILE ELIMINATING THE NEED FOR CHEMICAL AND POST-TREATMENT ASSOCIATED WITH CONVENTIONAL TREATMENT METHODS. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROGRAM INCLUDE SOURCE AND DRINKING WATER MANAGERS AND COMMUNITIES THAT EXPERIENCE MC-LR BLOOMS.
